sources.title: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback
supported_statements: Self-Refine is an agent or LLM self-improvement method focused on iterative refinement through self-feedback.
ASIEP should be positioned as an evidence layer beside self-improvement algorithms, not as a replacement algorithm.
caveats: Preprint or paper page status should be rechecked before submission.
sources.notes: Used for related-work positioning of self-improvement methods.

sources.title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
supported_statements: Reflexion is a self-reflection and feedback-based agent method.
ASIEP does not claim to replace behavioral improvement methods.
caveats: Publication and peer-review status should be checked before a camera-ready paper.

sources.title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
supported_statements: Voyager is an open-ended embodied agent work involving iterative skill acquisition.
ASIEP is narrower: it records accountability evidence for self-improvement events.
caveats: Use as background for agent self-improvement rather than direct standards evidence.
sources.notes: Used for related-work contrast with agent behavior improvement.

sources.title: AgentDevel: Reframing Self-Evolving LLM Agents as Release Engineering
supported_statements: AgentDevel is a directly related self-evolving agent and release-engineering reference point.
ASIEP should be framed as complementary evidence accountability, not as a replacement for AgentDevel.
caveats: Preprint status and final publication venue should be rechecked before submission.
sources.notes: Most direct related work for positioning.

sources.title: Semantic conventions for generative AI systems
supported_statements: OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions are documented by OpenTelemetry.
The GenAI semantic conventions page was marked Development at access time.
caveats: Specification status can change; recheck close to submission.
sources.notes: Used for trace-source and standard-status claims.

sources.title: Semantic Conventions for GenAI agent and framework spans
supported_statements: OpenTelemetry lists GenAI operation names such as create_agent, invoke_agent, and execute_tool in agent span conventions.
The relevant operation values are marked Development at access time.
caveats: Development status means ASIEP should not claim stable OTel conformance.
sources.notes: Used to justify OTel-like fixture mapping without claiming collector integration.

sources.title: LangSmith Observability concepts
supported_statements: LangSmith documents trace and run concepts for observability.
LangSmith is product documentation, not an open standard.
caveats: Product documentation can change without stable standards versioning.
sources.notes: Used for LangSmith-like trace fixture positioning.

sources.title: Log user feedback using the SDK
supported_statements: LangSmith documents feedback attached to traces or runs.
ASIEP's LangSmith-like fixture is a local abstraction over documented trace and feedback concepts.
caveats: ASIEP does not call the LangSmith API in M8.
sources.notes: Used for feedback mapping in related work.

sources.title: PROV-DM: The PROV Data Model
supported_statements: PROV-DM is a W3C Recommendation and a domain-agnostic provenance data model.
PROV-DM core concepts include entities, activities, and agents.
caveats: PROV provides provenance structure but not ASIEP-specific gate, repair, or bundle policies.

sources.title: PROV-O: The PROV Ontology
supported_statements: PROV-O maps PROV concepts to an OWL2 ontology.
PROV-O supports JSON-LD/RDF-oriented provenance alignment.
caveats: ASIEP uses PROV-like JSON-LD locally and does not claim complete PROV-O reasoning.

sources.title: Recording provenance of workflow runs with RO-Crate
supported_statements: Workflow Run RO-Crate records workflow run provenance using RO-Crate metadata.
ASIEP package metadata should be framed as local RO-Crate-like metadata unless externally validated.
caveats: ASIEP does not claim full Workflow Run RO-Crate certification.
sources.notes: Used with Process Run Crate profile documentation.

sources.title: Process Run Crate profile
supported_statements: Process Run Crate profile uses RO-Crate metadata to describe process execution.
The profile mentions SoftwareApplication and CreateAction entities for run provenance.
caveats: ASIEP only creates local RO-Crate-like metadata in M5.
sources.notes: Canonical profile source for process-run packaging comparison.

sources.title: FAIR Digital Objects Architecture Specification
supported_statements: FDO architecture describes FAIR Digital Object building blocks for machine-actionable objects.
ASIEP's M5 output is a local FDO-like record and does not claim registry registration or a global PID.
caveats: Architecture source was draft-like at access time; recheck status before submission.
sources.notes: Used for local FDO-like boundary language.

sources.title: AI Agent Standards Initiative
supported_statements: NIST has launched an AI Agent Standards Initiative focused on trustworthy, interoperable, and secure agent ecosystems.
This is a timing and motivation signal, not evidence that ASIEP has been adopted by NIST.
caveats: Do not state or imply NIST adoption of ASIEP.

sources.title: EU AI Act Article 12: Record-keeping
sources.organization: European Commission AI Act Service Desk
supported_statements: EU AI Act Article 12 requires high-risk AI systems to technically allow automatic recording of events over their lifetime.
Article 12 connects logging to traceability and post-market monitoring.
caveats: ASIEP should be presented as governance logging motivation, not as legal compliance.
sources.notes: Used for accountability and traceability motivation.

sources.title: EU AI Act Article 72: Post-market monitoring by providers and post-market monitoring plan for high-risk AI systems
sources.organization: European Commission AI Act Service Desk
supported_statements: EU AI Act Article 72 addresses post-market monitoring for high-risk AI systems.
ASIEP may use post-market monitoring as governance motivation but not as proof of compliance.
caveats: Do not claim ASIEP satisfies Article 72 obligations.

sources.title: OWASP Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications
sources.version: OWASP GenAI Security Project page accessed 2026-05-05
supported_statements: OWASP GenAI security materials identify and document security risks for LLM and agentic applications.
ASIEP should use OWASP as threat background, not as evidence that it solves agent security.
caveats: OWASP material is security guidance, not ASIEP validation.
sources.notes: Used for security-threat background and reviewer-risk framing.

sources.title: SLSA Provenance
supported_statements: SLSA provenance describes verifiable information about how software artifacts were produced.
SLSA provides a software supply-chain provenance analogy for ASIEP, not a direct agent accountability solution.
caveats: ASIEP does not claim SLSA compliance.
